analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet announces that EU HRVP Josep Borrell has issued a statement concerning the announced U.S. sanctions against the International Criminal Court (ICC). It implies EU institutional engagement on a rule-of-law/human-rights issue but provides no content of the statement. Evaluation: EU1 (successful story) – Not promoted; no reference to EU history or overcoming obstacles. EU2 (EU is united) – Not evident; no mention of member state unity or collective position. EU3 (useful for citizens, internal) – Not applicable; this concerns external/foreign policy and international justice, not internal citizen-focused benefits. EU4 (avant-garde actor) – Not supported; no claim of pioneering regulation or leadership in future-oriented policy. EU5 (global champion of fair causes) – Potentially relevant theme (defense of international justice/human rights), but the tweet does not express any value judgment or stance—only notes the existence of a statement—so it does not clearly promote this narrative. EU6 (contributes to a better world) – Similarly, while the topic touches multilateralism/ICC, the tweet contains no explicit assertion of EU’s global contribution. Conclusion: Lacking substantive content or framing, the tweet does not clearly promote any listed narrative.
